title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure and Program Overview
subtitle: Continuity, stewardship, host structure, and program design
summary: Continuity, stewardship functions, host structure, international coordination, and program design define the infrastructure question for Sutton SignWriting.

slides.title: What This Series Asks
slides.summary: Existing SignWriting infrastructure needs a stewardship structure that keeps it usable, maintained, and cumulative.

image_prompts.concrete: Governance overview slide showing domains, code, data, fonts, standards, documentation, coordination, and succession planning under a stewardship structure
image_prompts.abstract: Infrastructure functions arranged under a stable stewardship frame

slides.speech: The stewardship question is practical: what structure would let existing SignWriting infrastructure remain usable, maintained, cumulative, and accountable over time?
slides.notes: Keep the tone governance-oriented, not urgent.

slides.title: Stewardship Functions
slides.summary: The series names concrete continuity functions instead of treating stewardship as a vague institutional wish.

image_prompts.concrete: Function map slide showing domains, servers, code, fonts, data, standards, documentation, partner coordination, and succession planning
image_prompts.abstract: Named infrastructure functions connected by continuity lines

slides.speech: The series works best when stewardship is named by function. Domains, code, data, fonts, standards, documentation, coordination, and succession planning are concrete responsibilities. That makes the stewardship case practical rather than rhetorical.
slides.notes: Keep the vocabulary functional and governance-oriented.

slides.title: Reading Path
slides.summary: Readers can start with the executive brief, then move into stewardship, host requirements, philanthropy, international coordination, and precedents.

image_prompts.concrete: Institutional reading path slide moving from Executive Brief to Infrastructure and Stewardship, Host Structure, Infrastructure Core, International Shared Support, and Precedents
image_prompts.abstract: A calm institutional path through stewardship documents

bullets: Executive Brief for the short case
International and philanthropy entries for models
slides.speech: The fastest path is the Executive Brief first. Then readers can move into Infrastructure and Stewardship, Host Structure and Stewardship Requirements, Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy, International Shared Support, and the precedents entry depending on their institutional role.
slides.notes: Make the route useful for grant officers, fiscal sponsors, and institutional readers.

slides.title: Accountable Models
slides.summary: Stewardship can be organized through a host, consortium, fiscal sponsor, or related accountable structure.

image_prompts.concrete: Accountable stewardship model slide showing host, consortium, fiscal sponsor, technical stewardship, data preservation, partner coordination, and succession planning
image_prompts.abstract: Several accountable structures connected to named stewardship functions

slides.speech: The model can vary, but the responsibilities need to be named. A host, consortium, fiscal sponsor, or related accountable structure should make technical stewardship, data preservation, partner coordination, and succession planning easier to sustain without implying authority that has not been formally granted.
slides.notes: Keep the models practical and bounded.
